name: scientific-writing
description: Core skill for the deep research and writing tool. Write scientific manuscripts in full paragraphs (never bullet points). Use two-stage process with (1) section outlines with key points using research-lookup then (2) convert to flowing prose. IMRAD structure, citations (APA/AMA/Vancouver), figures/tables, reporting guidelines (CONSORT/STROBE/PRISMA), for research papers and journal submissions.
capabilities: Guide papers through Introduction, Methods, Results, and Discussion (IMRAD) structure
Apply citation styles including APA, AMA, Vancouver, Chicago, and IEEE
Create figures and tables following publication-quality standards
Ensure compliance with reporting guidelines (CONSORT, STROBE, PRISMA)
Format abstracts and manuscripts according to journal requirements
Write in full paragraphs using two-stage process from outline to prose
author: K-Dense-AI
securitySummary: Documentation-only skill containing markdown files and LaTeX templates. All 846 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES triggered by benign documentation syntax: markdown backticks for inline code formatting, LaTeX file path references, algorithm term references in text, and documentation URLs. No executable code exists. No security concerns present.
valueStatement: This skill guides the creation of publication-ready scientific manuscripts. It provides structured approaches for IMRAD format, proper citation styles, figure and table guidelines, and compliance with reporting standards like CONSORT, STROBE, and PRISMA.
userTitle: Write Scientific Manuscripts with IMRAD Structure
limitations: Does not perform actual literature searches (requires research-lookup skill)
Does not generate or analyze experimental data
Does not submit manuscripts to journals or interact with submission systems
Does not verify citation accuracy against original sources

useCases.title: Draft Research Papers
useCases.description: Structure and write complete research manuscripts following IMRAD format with proper citations and figures.

useCases.title: Format Thesis and Dissertations
useCases.description: Apply scientific writing principles and formatting guidelines for academic thesis preparation.

useCases.title: Prepare Clinical Reports
useCases.description: Create clinical reports following CONSORT guidelines and journal-specific formatting requirements.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Manuscript Structure
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me write the Introduction section for a research paper about [topic]. Use IMRAD structure and include guidance on establishing research context and identifying knowledge gaps.

promptTemplates.title: Citation Formatting
promptTemplates.prompt: Format these references in [APA/AMA/Vancouver] style: [list of citations]. Show me the in-text citation format and reference list entry for each.

promptTemplates.title: Reporting Guidelines
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my [CONSORT/STROBE/PRISMA] checklist and help me ensure my [randomized trial/observational study/systematic review] manuscript includes all required elements.

promptTemplates.title: Full Manuscript
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me write a complete research paper on [topic] using IMRAD structure. Start by creating an outline with key points, then expand each section into flowing prose paragraphs. Include placeholder citations and figure references.

bestPractices: Always write in full paragraphs with flowing prose - never submit bullet points in the final manuscript
Use the two-stage process: create outlines with research-lookup first, then convert to complete paragraphs
Follow journal-specific author guidelines for formatting, word limits, and section requirements
Include comprehensive citations from primary sources published within the last 5-10 years
antiPatterns: Leaving bullet points in the final manuscript instead of converting to prose
Using the same citation style inconsistently throughout the document
Overloading with jargon without defining specialized terms for the target audience
Skipping reporting guidelines when applicable to your study type

faq.question: What citation styles are supported?
faq.answer: APA, AMA, Vancouver, Chicago, IEEE, ACS, and NLM styles are supported. Choose based on your target journal's requirements.

faq.question: What is the two-stage writing process?
faq.answer: Stage 1: Create outlines with bullet points using research-lookup. Stage 2: Convert outlines to full prose paragraphs.

faq.question: Which reporting guidelines apply to my study?
faq.answer: CONSORT for trials, STROBE for observational studies, PRISMA for reviews, STARD for diagnostics, ARRIVE for animal research.

faq.question: How many figures should a paper include?
faq.answer: Minimum 5-6 figures for research papers: graphical abstract, methods flowchart, results visualizations, and conceptual diagrams.

faq.question: Does this skill search for literature?
faq.answer: No, this skill focuses on writing. Use the research-lookup skill for literature searches, then apply this skill for writing.

faq.question: Can this skill format for specific journals?
faq.answer: This skill provides general scientific writing. Use the venue-templates skill for journal-specific formatting and style guides.
